patch.value: GitHub analyzer follow-up: GH-628 unnecessary spread, GH-629 duplicate blocks, and GH-630 unsafe optional chaining

Current GitHub CodeFactor reports filed 2026-07-21: https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/628, https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/629, and https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/issues/630. Reproduce against current main, remediate the exact findings without suppressions, and close the GitHub issues with focused plus repo-wide quality evidence.

All three current analyzer findings are reproduced and fixed without suppression; focused tests cover behavioral risk; pnpm lint and full 100/100/100/100 coverage pass; GH-628, GH-629, and GH-630 are closed by the merged fix.

value.author: codex-derived-context-20260721
value.text: Duplicate check (all statuses, 2026-07-21): exact searches for GH-628, GH-629, and GH-630 returned no items. Reuses parent pm-92if and relates historical class owners pm-zt1c (spread), pm-fmjy (duplication), and pm-m8yl (unsafe optional chaining) instead of reopening completed work.

patch.value: GH-629 canonical project context field block

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation evidence: GH-628 replaces the unnecessary graph result spread with direct cache annotation; GH-629 centralizes project-context field keys in ITEM_PROJECT_CONTEXT_KEYS and reuses them in structured mutations; GH-630 adds an explicit committed-result guard before structuredContent access. GitHub issues #628/#629/#630 each received a direct pm-aw59hb link. Typecheck, strict static quality, 100% coverage, and 205 focused SDK/graph/mutation tests pass.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted static follow-up: CI exposed four ESLint-only findings not included in the earlier composite command. Removed redundant copy warning initializers, documented the two intentional best-effort cache catches, and pruned the now-obsolete no-useless-assignment suppression. Exact hosted command parity now passes locally: pnpm lint (ESLint, zero jscpd clones, CodeFactor/static/docstrings/package boundary/token budget) plus focused copy/index tests 9/9.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted CI finding: coverage shard 4/4 caught release-contract drift after the obsolete ESLint suppression was pruned. The live suppression count is 150 while MAX_ESLINT_SUPPRESSIONS and package-script assertions remained 151. Tightening the pinned baseline to 150 across the gate, package script, and contract tests before the next push.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted CI fix verified locally: release automation/readiness/static-quality contracts pass 115/115, pnpm lint passes with zero clones and strict static/docstring/package/token gates, and pnpm typecheck passes. The suppression budget is now pinned to the exact live baseline of 150 so future growth cannot hide behind the stale allowance.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR #636 exact-head review evidence at e0ae27f0: all hosted checks pass, including merged 100% coverage, static quality, CodeQL, Semgrep, Trivy, CodeFactor, DeepScan, Windows, docs, smokes, and CodSpeed. All six CodeRabbit threads were individually reacted to and replied to; valid findings are fixed and every thread is resolved. Gemini is sunset, Sourcery and CodeRabbit reported quota/rate limits after completion, and Greptile produced no review artifact despite repeated triggers. PR #636 declares Closes #628/#629/#630 so GitHub will close all three on merge.

patch.value: Implemented and verified GH-628/GH-629/GH-630 in PR #636 with exact-head green checks, 100% coverage, and resolved bot review feedback; the PR closes all three GitHub issues on merge.

author: codex-root

GitHub analyzer findings GH-628, GH-629, and GH-630 are eliminated without suppressions; focused and repository-wide gates remain green.
PR #636 exact head passes all hosted checks and 100% coverage; all CodeRabbit findings are resolved; the PR closes GH-628, GH-629, and GH-630 on merge.
